commit.message:
fix: deep audit — Date serialization, memory leak, z-index, error leakage

- Fix critical Date serialization bug: all persisted types now use ISO
  strings instead of Date objects (29 files). After page reload, dates
  were silently strings but typed as Date — causing subtle runtime bugs.
- Fix useToast memory leak: dependency array [state] caused unbounded
  listener growth on every state change. Changed to [].
- Fix z-index overflow: llm-selector used zIndex 9999, changed to 100
  to match project's z-index scale.
- Fix API error leakage: completions and providers routes no longer
  expose raw error messages to clients. Errors logged server-side.
- Fix flipping-card hardcoded colors: replaced neutral-*/bg-white with
  theme-aware border-border/bg-card/text-foreground.
- Add try-catch safety to connectors-panel localStorage parse.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Supabase user accounts — auth, profile storage, and session persistence

Backend:
- Install @supabase/supabase-js
- backend/services/authService.js: Supabase client singleton + per-request clientForToken() helper
- backend/middleware/requireAuth.js: validates Supabase JWT from Authorization header, attaches req.user
- backend/routes/users.js: GET /api/users/profile (create-on-first-access) and PUT /api/users/preferences
- backend/migrations/001_create_users_table.sql: users table DDL with RLS policies and signup trigger
- backend/.env.example: add SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY

Frontend:
- Install @supabase/supabase-js
- frontend/src/services/supabase.js: Supabase client initialised from VITE_SUPABASE_* env vars
- frontend/src/context/AuthContext.jsx: user/session state, signUp, signIn, signInWithGoogle, signOut; persists session via onAuthStateChange
- frontend/src/pages/Auth.jsx: sign in / sign up toggle, email+password form, Google OAuth button
- frontend/src/App.jsx: wrap in AuthProvider, add /auth route, sync Supabase profile into app state on login
- frontend/src/components/Navigation.jsx: show user email + sign-out when logged in; Login button when logged out (desktop + mobile)
- frontend/src/pages/StorePicker.jsx: save selected store to Supabase via PUT /api/users/preferences when logged in
- frontend/src/components/PreferencesPanel.jsx: save dietary_restrictions + excluded_ingredients to Supabase on Apply when logged in
- frontend/src/services/api.js: inject Supabase JWT into every request; add usersApi.getProfile() and usersApi.updatePreferences()
- frontend/.env.example: add VITE_SUPABASE_URL and VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add stress testing framework: defensive fixes, chaos proxy, serial analyzer

Firmware hardening:
- Zero-init newFlights[] and each Flight struct before population
- Null/empty guards in getAirline() and alreadyLogged()
- Heap fragmentation guard: skip proxy when maxBlock < 8KB
- Log maxBlock alongside free heap in fetch diagnostics

Testing infrastructure:
- 3 new mock proxy modes (chaos, transition, flap) for resilience testing
- Serial log analyzer (tools/serial-stress.js) with PASS/FAIL verdict
- build.sh stress command: automated chaos test pipeline
- build.sh proxy-host command: patch PROXY_HOST + compile + flash
- 6 new test cases + 5 fixture files for edge cases (null fields, overflow)

Verified: 10-min chaos test passed with 0 reboots, stable heap ~194KB.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(client): auth page crash on stale chunk / server error after deploy

Root causes:
1. ChunkLoadError — after a new deploy, React.lazy() tries to load old
   chunk URLs (from cached index.html) which 404. The error propagated
   through <Suspense> → ErrorBoundary → "Что-то пошло не так" screen.
   Fix: ErrorBoundary detects ChunkLoadError by message/name and does a
   hard window.location.reload() once (guarded by sessionStorage flag to
   prevent infinite loops).

2. fetchUser() threw on non-401 server errors (5xx, network failure),
   leaving the auth page with an error query state instead of showing
   the login form. Fix: wrap fetch in try/catch, return null on any
   non-2xx or network error — unauthenticated is the safe fallback.

Tests: tests/e2e/auth-401.spec.ts — three Playwright cases mock 401,
500, and network abort on /api/auth/user and assert login form renders.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

BI V2: Grade-System recalibriert + Close-Entry als Hauptfilter

Alte Grade-Schwellen waren unrealistisch (S>=170 von 200 = 85% — unerreichbar).
Kein einziger S/A-Grade Trade in 6 Monaten gefunden.

Recalibriert:
  S: 170→140 (70%) | A: 140→120 (60%) | B: 110→100 (50%) | C: 80 (bleibt)
  Threshold: Long 100→85, Short 90→80

Grade C wieder zugelassen weil:
  - Close-based Entry ist der echte Fake-Breakout-Filter (nicht Grade)
  - 5 Trades in 6 Monaten = statistisch nutzlos
  - Mehr Trades noetig fuer aussagekraeftige Ergebnisse

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove prodMigrations to fix build hang permanently

Dev mode pushes schema directly to the DB, so prodMigrations is
redundant and causes interactive prompts when it finds batch=-1
dev records. Without prodMigrations, the production build skips
migrate() entirely — the schema is already in sync from dev pushes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Phase 4 gap analysis (v2.0): audit closed gaps, identify 7 new gaps

Closes/tracks 18 original gaps from v1.0:
- 7 fully closed (G-001 through G-007, G-010) in Phases 1-3
- 4 remaining P2 open (G-008/009/011–014)
- 4 remaining P3 open (G-015/016/018)
- 2 residual partial (G-004 startup soft-degradation, G-017 pickle fallback)

New gaps discovered during Phase 2+3 implementation audit:
- G-019 (P1): Ground truth corpus too small — 3 artificials cannot measure sigma-5 FPR
- G-020 (P1): Bayesian posterior ceiling (~4%) not documented or surfaced to operators
- G-021 (P2): Multi-source enrichment is SBDB-only — NEODyS/MPC unimplemented, Horizons uninstantiated
- G-022 (P2): EMERGENCY suppressions in advanced_scoring.py mask unknown config issue
- G-023 (P2): aneos_menu.py 10,781-line monolith has 265 untested functions
- G-024 (P2): Temporal analysis stuck at single epoch (Horizons not wired)
- G-025 (P3): AI annotator may overstate confidence relative to Bayesian posterior

Includes Phase 4 sequencing: 4A scientific accuracy, 4B data sources,
4C architecture, 4D API quality, 4E future capabilities.

feat(ego-lint): add D-Bus connectSignal leak detection (R-LIFE-25) (#59)

## Summary

- Add R-LIFE-25: detect bare `proxy.connectSignal()` calls without
stored return value and no auto-cleanup mechanism
(`connectObject`/`disconnectObject`/`SignalTracker`)
- D-Bus signal handlers accumulate across enable/disable cycles, causing
duplicate callbacks and memory leaks
- Follows R-LIFE-21 (GSettings signal leak) template — same per-file
scanning, extension-wide auto-cleanup detection, and skip patterns
- Also adds `connectSignal` to `build-resource-graph.py` CREATE_PATTERNS
for resource graph visibility

## Evidence

Media Controls field test annotation confirmed D-Bus signal leaks
(`proxy.connectSignal('NameOwnerChanged'|'PropertiesChanged'|'Seeked')`)
as a true positive found only by ego-review, not caught by ego-lint.

## Test plan

- [x] `./ego-lint tests/fixtures/dbus-signal-leak@test --verbose` → FAIL
on `lifecycle/dbus-signal-leak`
- [x] `./ego-lint tests/fixtures/dbus-signal-auto-cleanup@test
--verbose` → PASS (auto-cleanup present)
- [x] `bash tests/run-tests.sh` → 590 passed, 0 failed

Closes #58

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(opensearch): correct OIDC discovery URL for container networking (CAB-1686) (#1550)

## Summary
- Fix OpenSearch security plugin `openid_connect_url` pointing to
`http://localhost/auth/...` which is unreachable from inside Docker
containers
- Changed to `http://keycloak:8080/...` (Docker service name), matching
the Dashboards config that already uses the correct URL
- All other OIDC infrastructure (KC client, Dashboards config, roles,
role mappings, multi-tenancy) was already correctly configured from
CAB-1114 Phase 3

## Context
The `openid_connect_url` in `config.yml` is used by the OpenSearch
backend to fetch the OIDC discovery document and validate JWT tokens.
Inside Docker, `localhost` refers to the container itself, not the
Docker host — so OpenSearch couldn't reach Keycloak for token
validation. The Dashboards config (`opensearch_dashboards.yml`) already
uses `http://keycloak:8080/...` correctly.

## Test plan
- [ ] `docker compose up` with OpenSearch + Keycloak + Dashboards
- [ ] Login to OpenSearch Dashboards via Keycloak OIDC flow
- [ ] cpi-admin user sees all indices
- [ ] tenant-admin user sees only own tenant indices

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Melody-range filter bank + sliding-window drone removal for sessions

Foreground path now uses three layers of accompaniment rejection:

1. HPSS: removes percussion (bodhran, guitar strums, foot tapping)
2. Melody-range filter bank: only uses frequency bins 250Hz-3.5kHz
   for chromagram extraction, excluding guitar/bouzouki/accordion bass
   that lives below 250Hz
3. Sliding-window drone removal: 4-second sliding median subtraction
   per chroma bin catches chord changes and pads, not just constant
   drones — handles bouzouki/guitar chord progressions

The melody-range restriction is the biggest win: Irish melody instruments
(whistle, flute, fiddle, concertina) sit primarily in 250Hz-3.5kHz,
while bass accompaniment lives below 250Hz.

commit.message:
feat: show UPI deep link button only on mobile devices

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Stale worker detection: identify stuck smiths via progress heartbeats (Forge-qa8) (#62)

* feat: stale worker detection via log file heartbeats (Forge-qa8)

Add stale worker detection that identifies stuck smiths by monitoring
log file modification times. Workers whose log files haven't been
modified within the configurable stale_interval (default 5m) are
marked with a new "stalled" status and surfaced in the Needs Attention
panel.

Changes:
- Add WorkerStalled status constant and EventWorkerStalled event type
- Add StalledWorkers() and MarkWorkerStalled() to state DB
- Add stale_interval config setting (default 5m, 0 disables)
- Add runStaleDetection goroutine in daemon (checks at half interval)
- Include stalled workers in NeedsAttentionBeads() query
- Include stalled status in ActiveWorkers and related queries
- Add comprehensive test for stale detection DB operations

This is distinct from timeout — timeout kills the process after
smith_timeout expires. Stale detection warns the operator before the
timeout fires, so they can investigate hung workers.

Bead: Forge-qa8

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-qa8

- db: add updated_at column to workers, set it in MarkWorkerStalled so
  stalled workers sort by actual stall time not original start time
- db: StalledWorkers now excludes already-stalled workers to avoid
  repeated filesystem stat calls on unchanged log files
- db: include 'stalled' status in ActiveDispatchWorkers queries so
  stalled workers continue to count against dispatch capacity
- db: NeedsAttentionBeads deduplication now merges flags (OR) when a
  bead appears in both retries and stalled branches, preferring the
  more actionable retry reason over the generic stalled message
- daemon: fix runStaleDetection interval when stale_interval < 30s so
  checkInterval never exceeds staleInterval, preventing delayed detection
- daemon: store config in atomic.Pointer[config.Config] and add
  config() accessor so hot-reload updates are race-free across goroutines

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-qa8

- daemon: reset stale detection ticker when stale_interval changes via hot-reload
- state: merge Title field when deduplicating NeedsAttentionBeads rows
- config: validate stale_interval is not negative and >= 30s when enabled

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-qa8

- StalledWorkers: return nil early when staleThreshold <= 0 to prevent
  accidental mass-stalling if caller passes zero
- daemon.runStaleDetection: remove redundant WorkerStalled status check
  since StalledWorkers already excludes stalled workers via SQL filter

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve CI failures for Forge-qa8

* fix: address review comments for Forge-qa8

* fix: address review comments for Forge-qa8

- Treat missing/unreadable log file as stalled when worker started before cutoff
- LEFT JOIN queue_cache in stalled-worker UNION branch for title enrichment
- Fix runStaleDetection doc comment: "will not run more frequently" vs "never less frequently"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve CI failures for Forge-qa8

* fix: address review comments for Forge-qa8

- Snapshot config once at start of pollAndDispatch to avoid race with hot-reload
- Add ok-check and empty-path guard for anvil config lookup
- Log error and treat as at-capacity when DispatchActiveCount fails
- Fix COALESCE title enrichment to use NULLIF to avoid empty string masking

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-qa8

- Use dbTimeLayout (not time.RFC3339) in MarkWorkerStalled and CompleteWorkersByBead
  to keep timestamps consistent and lexicographically orderable in SQLite
- Fix d.cfg.Settings -> d.config().Settings (atomic.Pointer needs Load())
- Fix NeedsAttentionBeads call in test to pass required threshold args

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-qa8

- Start runStaleDetection unconditionally so hot-reloading stale_interval
  from 0 to a positive value takes effect without a daemon restart
- Simplify runStaleDetection: remove dead-code branches for stale_interval
  < 30s (unreachable since config validation enforces >= 30s when enabled);
  goroutine now idles at 30s poll rate when disabled to react to re-enablement

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(smd): 8-point UX improvement - mobile, popup, social, compare, watermarks

1. Mobile responsive: shop product grid 1-col at 375px (grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2)
2. Style Quiz: images verified present (TAILORED & SHARP, SOFT & ROMANTIC .jpg exist)
3. Newsletter popup: added scroll 50% trigger alongside 30s timer
4. Prices: all products already show $ prefix in new design
5. Social links: removed fake Instagram link from footer (SMD uses Pinterest+Twitter only)
6. Compare page: created /compare index page, fixed nav link from /compare/zara-vs-hm to /compare
7. Watermarks: removed StyleMeDaily watermark from 75 SVG guide images + generator script
8. Build verified: 0 errors, 46 static pages generated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: accept pandas DataFrames as input across all estimators

Adds ensure_polars() helper that converts pandas DataFrames to Polars
on the fly. Pandas remains an optional dependency — only imported when
a pandas DataFrame is actually passed. 10 tests verify parity across
all estimators (OLS, IV, LIML, GMM, panel FE/RE/FD).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor: Shift SEO focus to AI-augmented engineering

Updated metadata to emphasize AI as core to your engineering practice:
- Changed primary title from Backend Engineer to AI-Augmented Systems Engineer
- Added AI-focused keywords (ai engineer, ai-augmented, prompt engineering, etc.)
- Updated descriptions to highlight AI collaboration approach
- Enhanced JSON-LD schema with AI expertise and knowledge areas
- Added LLM Integration and Prompt Engineering as known domains
- Reordered expertise to lead with AI-Augmented Development

This better reflects your unique approach of using AI as a collaborator
for system design, rapid prototyping, and infrastructure experimentation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: strip all auth scaffolding — restore plain open UI

Remove incomplete auth layer (AUTH_TOKEN, roles, sanitization,
login modal, view-only CSS). Board is fully open with no auth
requirements. Clean slate for proper server-side auth later.
